title: --device does not preserve group ownership in rootless --userns=keep-id
body:
### Issue Description

When using `--device /dev/XXX` with `--userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups`, device nodes are created with nobody:nobody ownership, ignoring the host's group.

This makes them unreadable in the guest, even thought all permissions align for it to be readable.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
podman run -i \
	--rm \
	--userns=keep-id \
	--group-add keep-groups \
	--device /dev/input/ \
	archlinux \
	ls -l /dev/input/event24
```






### Describe the results you received

Output:

```
crw-rw---- 1 nobody nobody 13, 88 Mar 26 00:21 /dev/input/event24
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected device to have the same permissions as in the host:

```
> ls -l /dev/input/event24
crw-rw----    1 root     plugdev    13,  88 Mar 26 01:21 /dev/input/event24
> id
…,105(plugdev),…
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

On Alpine Linux Edge.

### Additional information

AFAIK, `--device` recreates nodes via mknod rather than bind-mounting, so host ownership is lost entirely.

--device is unusable for any device that relies on group permissions.

Device in question is a gamepad; access is controlled via group memebership.

comments.body:
Rootless, in fact, bind mounts a device. Ownership isn't really _lost_, it's that the mapping is unavailable inside the user namespace (`--keep-id` doesn't help here since you're running as rootless, and your device is owned by root), and hence shows ownership as `nobody:nobody`.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/9bea6e759a4dc054daa45191d240f529542e2337/pkg/specgen/generate/config_linux.go#L145-L150

From #23571: if your rootless user is able to write to the device (via the plugdev group), then `--group-add keep-groups` should've worked. Can you confirm if the device indeed works despite the `nobody:nobody` ownership?

It's not really recommended for security reasons, but if you must, you could add a subgid entry for your plugdev user.

Confirmed, opening the device fails:

```console
> podman run -i --rm --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/input/ archlinux cat /dev/input/event24
cat: /dev/input/event24: Permission denied
> podman run -i --rm --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/input/ python:alpine python -c 'open("/dev/input/event24")' 
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
    open("/dev/input/event24")
    ~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/dev/input/event24'
```

On the host, the file is readable by me:

```console
> cat /dev/input/event24
(lots of gibberish)
```

On the host, only two devices are owned by `plugdev`:

```console
> ls -l /dev/input 
total 0
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  64 Apr 16 02:12 event0
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  65 Apr 16 02:12 event1
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  74 Apr 16 02:12 event10
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  75 Apr 16 02:12 event11
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  76 Apr 16 02:12 event12
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  77 Apr 16 02:12 event13
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  78 Apr 16 02:12 event14
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  79 Apr 16 02:12 event15
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  80 Apr 16 02:12 event16
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  81 Apr 16 02:12 event17
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  82 Apr 16 02:12 event18
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  83 Apr 16 02:12 event19
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  66 Apr 16 02:12 event2
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  84 Apr 16 02:12 event20
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  85 Apr 17 23:34 event21
crw-rw----    1 root     plugdev    13,  86 Apr 21 23:28 event22
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  87 Apr 17 01:15 event23
crw-rw----    1 root     plugdev    13,  88 Apr 21 23:28 event24
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  67 Apr 16 02:12 event3
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  68 Apr 16 02:12 event4
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  69 Apr 16 02:12 event5
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  70 Apr 16 02:12 event6
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  71 Apr 16 02:12 event7
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  72 Apr 16 02:12 event8
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  73 Apr 16 02:12 event9
crw-rw----    1 root     plugdev    13,   0 Apr 16 21:41 js0
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  63 Apr 16 02:12 mice
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  32 Apr 16 02:12 mouse0
crw-rw----    1 root     input      13,  33 Apr 21 21:51 mouse1
```

But in the container, all device nodes are owned by the same gid, and the user in the container is only a member of a single group:

```console
> podman run -it --rm --userns=keep-id --group-add keep-groups --device /dev/input/ archlinux bash
[hugo@92228ead8974 /]$ ls -ln /dev/input
total 0
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 64 Apr 16 00:12 event0
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 65 Apr 16 00:12 event1
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 74 Apr 16 00:12 event10
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 75 Apr 16 00:12 event11
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 76 Apr 16 00:12 event12
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 77 Apr 16 00:12 event13
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 78 Apr 16 00:12 event14
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 79 Apr 16 00:12 event15
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 80 Apr 16 00:12 event16
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 81 Apr 16 00:12 event17
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 82 Apr 16 00:12 event18
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 83 Apr 16 00:12 event19
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 66 Apr 16 00:12 event2
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 84 Apr 16 00:12 event20
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 85 Apr 17 21:34 event21
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 86 Apr 21 21:28 event22
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 87 Apr 16 23:15 event23
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 88 Apr 21 21:28 event24
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 67 Apr 16 00:12 event3
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 68 Apr 16 00:12 event4
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 69 Apr 16 00:12 event5
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 70 Apr 16 00:12 event6
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 71 Apr 16 00:12 event7
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 72 Apr 16 00:12 event8
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 73 Apr 16 00:12 event9
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13,  0 Apr 16 19:41 js0
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 63 Apr 16 00:12 mice
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 32 Apr 16 00:12 mouse0
crw-rw---- 1 65534 65534 13, 33 Apr 21 19:51 mouse1
[hugo@92228ead8974 /]$ id
uid=1000(hugo) gid=1000(hugo) groups=1000(hugo)
```

`--group-add keep-groups` is a crun only feature.

Try using crun instead of runc

Using `crun` the device nodes won't show in in the container. E.g.: `/dev/input/event24` doesn't exist. 

If I terminate the container and start a new one, the nodes _do_ show up. Containers need to be terminated and re-started every time a device change happens.
Perhaps `--group-add keep-groups` should fail with an error if using `runc`? It's currently silently ignored, and then things fail in mysterious ways (such as this issue).

> Using `crun` the device nodes won't show in in the container. E.g.: `/dev/input/event24` doesn't exist.
> 
> If I terminate the container and start a new one, the nodes _do_ show up. Containers need to be terminated and re-started every time a device change happens.

that was a regression in 1.27.

Can you try 1.27.1 or even an older one?

```
> podman info --format '{{.Host.OCIRuntime.Name}}'                                                              
crun
> podman info --format '{{.Host.OCIRuntime.Version}}'
crun version 1.27.1
commit: 3ec076b3b6714ec2f1a10533cf18d5605a6de637
rundir: /tmp/rundir.1000.1bb5420a65041249/crun
spec: 1.0.0
+SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
```
why not using directly a bind mount like `-v /dev/input:/dev/input`?
